title: Spherical bed topping/hold-down media for an autothermal reformation/secondary reformation unit
abstract: A generally spherical high strength ceramic body for use in an ATR and/or SR unit covering a catalyst media bed. The ceramic body is a fully stabilized YSZ composite composition having at least about 13 % yttria YSZ, and more typically from about 12% to about 20% yttria YSZ, with a porosity of less than 20 percent and a diameter of at least 25 mm and, more typically, selected from the group comprising 25 mm, 50mm, 76 mm and 100 mm.
claims: Claims Claims: I claim: 1. A generally spherical ceramic body, comprising: a generally spherical refractory body; wherein the generally spherical body has a diameter selected from the group comprising 38 mm, 50mm, 76 mm, and 100 mm; wherein the body is YSZ; wherein the body has a yttria content of about thirteen weight percent yttria with the balance being substantially zirconia; wherein the YSZ is fully stabilized. 2. The body of claim 1 wherein the body has a porosity of less than twenty percent. 3. The body of claim 1 wherein the body is sintered. 4. The body of claim 1 wherein the body is cast. 5. The body of claim 1 wherein the body has a void fraction of less than 3 percent. 6. An inert catalyst support hold down bed, comprising: a plurality of generally spherical refractory bodies defining an intermediate bed positioned between a burner and a catalyst media bed; and network of air passages infiltrating the intermediate bed; wherein each respective generally spherical body has a diameter selected from the group comprising 25 mm, 50mm, 76 mm, and 100 mm; wherein each respective generally spherical body has a fully stabilized YSZ content of about thirteen weight percent; wherein each respective generally spherical body has a void fraction of less than 20 percent. 7. A high strength ceramic body for use in a covering layer over a SR/ATR catalyst media bed, comprising: a generally spherical portion; wherein the ceramic body has a void fraction of less than 5 percent; wherein the ceramic body has a diameter selected from the group comprising 25 mm, 50mm, and 76 mm; and wherein the ceramic body is YSZ having a yttria content between 12 weight percent and 16 weight percent. 8. A method of retarding the degradation of refractory metal oxide material in a gas reforming burner, comprising: a) positioning a catalyst media bed in a reforming burner chamber; b) covering the catalyst media bed with plurality of inert refractory balls; c) flowing hot gas from a flame source through the plurality of inert refractory balls to slow and distribute the flowing hot gas to yield a slowed gas; and d) flowing the slowed gas through the catalyst media bed to catalyze the slowed gas to yield a synthesis gas product; wherein each respective inert refractory ball is at least 25 mm in diameter; and wherein each respective inert refractory ball is between 8 weight percent and 20 weight percent yttria; and wherein each inert refractory ball is fully stabilized YSZ. 9. The method of claim 8 wherein the respective inert refractory balls have diameters selected from the group comprising 25 mm, 50 mm, and 76 mm. 10. The method of claim 8 wherein the respective inert refractory balls contain 13 percent fully stabilized YSZ.
